machinery_genes.role: polytopic inner-membrane protein; secreted/required for substrate translocation
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020, Table 1: DotA identified by mass spectrometry in the isolated Dot/Icm T4SS complex from L. pneumophila Lp02 (a Philadelphia-1 derivative). DotA is one of the originally named dot virulence genes (Vogel 1998).

machinery_genes.role: cytoplasmic ATPase (energizer; VirB11/PilT-like)
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020, Table 1: DotB present in the isolated Dot/Icm complex. DotB is the VirB11/DotB-family cytoplasmic ATPase of the Dot/Icm machine.

machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane core-complex lipoprotein (OMC component)
machinery_genes.quote: Central to assembly of the apparatus are five proteins that define the core complex: DotC, DotD, DotF, DotG, and DotH
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020 names DotC as one of the five core-complex proteins; modeled in the OMC disk of the cryo-EM structure.

machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane core-complex lipoprotein (VirB7 homolog)
machinery_genes.quote: Central to assembly of the apparatus are five proteins that define the core complex: DotC, DotD, DotF, DotG, and DotH
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020: DotD modeled in the OMC disk; its fold resembles VirB7 homologs of other T4SSs (two copies, DotD and DotD).

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane / core-complex component (couples OMC to periplasmic ring)
machinery_genes.quote: Central to assembly of the apparatus are five proteins that define the core complex: DotC, DotD, DotF, DotG, and DotH
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020 lists DotF among the five core proteins; biochemically an integral inner-membrane protein that also associates with the outer membrane. icm synonym icmG.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane-to-outer-membrane channel ring (VirB10/CagY homolog; forms the dome)
machinery_genes.quote: Central to assembly of the apparatus are five proteins that define the core complex: DotC, DotD, DotF, DotG, and DotH
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020: DotG is the VirB10/CagY-homologous core protein; the C-terminus is proposed to form the OMC dome. icm synonym icmE. A dotG deletion abolishes the dome and periplasmic ring.

machinery_genes.role: periplasmic core-complex component (VirB9 homolog)
machinery_genes.quote: Within the disk, we unambiguously traced and identified DotC, DotD, DotK and DotH along with the protein Lpg0657
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020: DotH modeled in the OMC; structurally a VirB9 homolog (closest to TraO). icm synonym icmK.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane assembly component (VirB8-like)
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020, Table 1: DotI present in the isolated complex. icm synonym icmL; VirB8-family inner-membrane component.

machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane-associated core-complex component (peptidoglycan-binding-like fold)
machinery_genes.quote: Within the disk, we unambiguously traced and identified DotC, DotD, DotK and DotH along with the protein Lpg0657
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020: DotK modeled in the OMC near the outer membrane; an unexpected core component. icm synonym icmN.

machinery_genes.role: type-IV coupling protein (substrate-receptor ATPase; VirD4 homolog)
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020, Table 1: DotL present in the isolated complex. DotL is the VirD4-family coupling protein (T4CP) of the Dot/Icm system. icm synonym icmO.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane component (Dot/Icm coupling/inner-membrane subcomplex with DotL/DotN)
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020, Table 1: DotM present in the isolated complex. icm synonym icmP.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane component of the coupling subcomplex
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020, Table 1: DotN present in the isolated complex. icm synonym icmJ.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane ATPase (VirB4 homolog; energizer)
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020, Table 1: DotO present in the isolated complex. DotO is the VirB4-family inner-membrane ATPase. icm synonym icmB.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane stabilizing component (DotU/TssL-like)
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020, Table 2 (delta-dotG complex): DotU present in the isolated Dot/Icm complex. icm synonym icmH; stabilizes the apparatus with IcmF.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane component (IcmF/TssM-like; stabilizes the apparatus)
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020, Table 1: IcmF present in the isolated complex. Inner-membrane stabilizing component, partners with DotU.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane component of the Dot/Icm apparatus
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020, Table 1: IcmV present in the isolated Dot/Icm complex.

machinery_genes.role: periplasmic component required for substrate translocation
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020, Table 1: IcmX present in the isolated complex; a periplasmic protein essential for Dot/Icm function.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane component required for pore formation / substrate release
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020, Table 1: IcmT present in the isolated complex. Independently named as a core icm gene by Segal 1998 (operon icmTS).

machinery_genes.role: cytoplasmic chaperone/adaptor of the Dot/Icm apparatus (IcmS-IcmW)
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020, Table 1: IcmS present in the isolated complex. IcmS-IcmW form a cytoplasmic adaptor that engages effectors at the coupling complex; included as machinery-associated, not as an effector. Named as core icm gene by Segal 1998 (operon icmTS).

machinery_genes.role: cytoplasmic chaperone/adaptor of the Dot/Icm apparatus (IcmS-IcmW)
machinery_genes.note: Durie 2020, Table 1: IcmW present in the isolated complex. Partner of IcmS in the cytoplasmic adaptor; machinery-associated, not an effector.

machinery_genes.role: pore-forming/membrane-associated component (with IcmR partner)
machinery_genes.quote: The 16 icm genes appeared to be organized as six individual genes (icmR, icmQ, icmG, icmC, icmD, and icmF), and four operons (icmTS, icmPO, icmMLKE, and icmJB)
machinery_genes.note: Segal, Purcell & Shuman 1998 (PNAS): icmQ named as one of the 16 icm genes required for macrophage killing. Membrane-interacting component of the Dot/Icm system.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane component of the Dot/Icm apparatus
machinery_genes.quote: The 16 icm genes appeared to be organized as six individual genes (icmR, icmQ, icmG, icmC, icmD, and icmF), and four operons (icmTS, icmPO, icmMLKE, and icmJB)
machinery_genes.note: Segal 1998 (PNAS): icmC named among the 16 macrophage-killing icm genes. dot synonym dotE.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane component of the Dot/Icm apparatus
machinery_genes.quote: The 16 icm genes appeared to be organized as six individual genes (icmR, icmQ, icmG, icmC, icmD, and icmF), and four operons (icmTS, icmPO, icmMLKE, and icmJB)
machinery_genes.note: Segal 1998 (PNAS): icmD named among the 16 macrophage-killing icm genes. dot synonym dotP.

notes: System = the Legionella pneumophila Dot/Icm type-IVB secretion system, the founding archetype of the Dot/Icm family (intracellular multiplication / defect in organelle trafficking). The two dual nomenclatures (dot and icm) were established by Vogel et al 1998 (Science, dot = defective for organelle trafficking) and Segal, Purcell & Shuman 1998 (PNAS, icm). Apparatus genes here are taken from those founding genetic papers and, primarily, from Durie et al 2020 (eLife) who purified the intact Dot/Icm T4SS core complex from L. pneumophila strain Lp02 (a thymidine-auxotroph derivative of the clinical isolate Philadelphia-1, i.e. the same strain background as this instance) and identified its components by cryo-EM and mass spectrometry (their Tables 1 and 2). Quotes of the form 'DotX\tQ5Z...' are verbatim rows from Durie Table 1/2 listing each Dot/Icm protein detected in the purified complex (UniProt accession alongside). Core channel/energetics covered: OMC core complex (DotC, DotD, DotF, DotG=VirB10, DotH=VirB9, DotK), coupling/inner-membrane ATPases (DotO=VirB4, DotB=VirB11, DotL=VirD4 coupling protein, DotM, DotN, DotI=VirB8), IM stabilizers (IcmF, DotU=IcmH), periplasmic IcmX, pore/translocation IcmT, IcmV, DotA, and IcmQ/IcmC/IcmD. IcmS and IcmW are included as machinery-associated cytoplasmic adaptor/chaperone components (they co-purify with the apparatus and form the substrate-engaging adaptor at the coupling complex), NOT as effectors. Deliberately excluded: the ~300 translocated Dot/Icm effectors (the instance's 56 curated effectors and the broader effector flood, which are genome-dispersed and GATED per the T4SS brief); the lvh/Lvh second T4SS of L. pneumophila (Segal 1999, PMID 10564519, dispensable for intracellular growth, a distinct system); IcmR (a cytoplasmic chaperone for IcmQ) and transcriptional regulators (CsrA, PmrAB, LetAS) as non-apparatus; Lpg0657 (co-purifies and structurally resembles DotK but is not an established dot/icm gene, so omitted as low-confidence). Some icm genes named only in Segal 1998 (e.g. icmG/icmJ/icmK/icmL/icmM/icmP/icmO/icmE) map onto the dot names already listed via the published dot<->icm synonymy and are therefore not double-counted. dotA, dotB, dotC, dotD are anchored by Vogel 1998 as the founding dot virulence genes; gene-level localization quotes drawn from Durie 2020. All quotes verbatim from the cited PMC full text (Durie eLife) or the PubMed abstract (Segal PNAS). Status COMPLETE: the core apparatus is documented gene-by-gene.
